execution_boundary.summary: Guardrails can constrain conversational and tool flows while external consequence execution requires a separate authority boundary.

preparation_boundary.summary: Flow and action controls can govern preparation before commitment.

commitment_boundary.summary: Guardrail state can inform commitment review without becoming execution authority.

semantic_equivalence_boundary.summary: Equivalent intents expressed through alternate conversational paths require consistent boundary treatment.

unknown_trajectory_boundary.summary: Unhandled flows, unknown actions, or model uncertainty require bounded handling.

authority_boundary.summary: Allowed conversational flow is not consequence-binding authority.

evidence_freshness_boundary.summary: Rails configuration, model, actions, and policy version matter at commit time.

reconstruction_boundary.summary: Conversation state, rails configuration, actions, model context, and decisions support reconstruction.

interoperability_path.summary: Guardrail state and decisions can enter a Commitment Candidate as runtime-control evidence.

next_required_action: add allowed-flow, blocked-flow, alternate-intent, unknown-action, and authority-drift fixtures
